intro: Minuartia muriculata, also known as Rough Alison, is a small, flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia muriculata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
